mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index signals expanding factory output and economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship holds across all observable time periods without requiring a threshold trigger.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns when INDPRO is above its 6-month moving average are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), or the proportion of positive returns is below 50% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals economic weakening and rising recession risk. This deterioration in labor market conditions leads to lower expected corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns in triggered months are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not negative with statistical significance (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3% and housing starts simultaneously fall below 1,200 thousand units, the joint signal indicates aggressive monetary tightening that directly harms housing-sensitive sectors. This interaction-driven contraction in credit-sensitive economic activity produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing downturn
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return during joint trigger events is not negative on average, or the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on main effects and interaction is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by CPI year-over-year growth exceeding 3%, rising consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations. Under this regime, elevated CPI directly translates to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism is inactive outside this regime.
failure_condition: During months when trailing 12-month CPI is above 3%, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL level with forward returns is not negative (IC < -0.05) over any 3-year window within the regime.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll employment growth exceeding 150,000 jobs signals robust labor demand, rising incomes, and consumer confidence. This positive labor market expansion immediately boosts near-term corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months following a payroll increase above 150k, the 5-day forward return is positive less than 55% of the time, or the average return is not statistically significant above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and real retail sales (RSAFS) concurrently exceed their respective 12-month moving averages, the joint expansion confirms broad-based economic momentum across manufacturing and consumer spending. This synchronized strength in supply and demand drives sustained corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS above their 12-month MA
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return in joint-trigger periods is not positive, or the proportion of positive returns is below 55% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the early expansion phase of the housing cycle, characterized by rising housing starts from low levels, increases in HOUST fuel construction activity, employment, and household wealth effects. This housing-led demand growth translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The positive relationship is regime-specific and does not hold in late-cycle or contractionary regimes.
regime_hint: early expansion phase with rising housing starts
failure_condition: In early expansion periods (HOUST rising from trough below 1000), the average 20-day forward return is not positive, or the correlation between HOUST level and forward returns is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP, as measured by quarterly GDPC1, reflects an expanding economy with rising output, employment, and profits. Higher economic output directly lifts corporate earnings over the long run, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is a persistent, always-on trend signal that does not require any additional trigger conditions.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day forward returns is not positive (Pearson r < 0.10) over any 10-year rolling window, or the average return in quarters with GDP growth above 2% is not significantly above zero.
